title: 'unknown' is not 'invalid' — it means the probe failed, not the address
summary: An `unknown` status reflects the SMTP probe, not the mailbox: the receiving server was unreachable, timed out, rate-limited, or greylisted. The address may be perfectly real. Don't discard `unknown` rows or fold them into 'bad' — surface them as 'couldn't determine, re-verify later'. Lowering smtp_timeout_secs trades speed for MORE unknowns (slow-but-real servers get cut off). Common causes: port 25 blocked at the destination, the verifier's IP being throttled, or transient greylisting that resolves on a retry minutes later.
references: docs/services/spiderverifyer/LEARNINGS.md:217-222 — mail-server probe detection raises unknown rates
